behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (three collectors -> aggregation payload), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required if each source client is replaced with in-process fakes returning distinct markers. LLM-DEP: this behavior checks the input to summary generation, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: no chained agentic decisions. Adversarial critique: if the real bug only appears with actual API response formats, Integration could be too low. TOP2-CONTRAST: runner-up System, but behavior text only requires 'content passed' includes all three sources; it does not require validating real network behavior. Rejection gate: System rejected because no concrete text requires real HTTP/timeouts/subprocesses; Agentic rejected because correctness does not depend on generated summary content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real data-collection orchestrator with three fake source adapters and a spy summary-generator boundary; assert the payload contains fields from metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log in one run.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: resilience behavior appears when source adapter errors propagate through report assembly and fallback formatting. REAL-INFRA: unavailability can be represented by adapter exception/empty response in-process; no mandatory real network failure needed by text. LLM-DEP: assertion is fallback note + continued report generation, not model judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent pipeline. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network proven by text, (b) no real AI output dependency, (c) no chained agentic steps, (d) no LLM-governed handoff. Adversarial critique: a real timeout/connection-reset path might differ from mocked errors. TOP2-CONTRAST: runner-up System, but behavior text says only 'unavailable' and expected graceful degradation, which integration-level injected failures can validate. Rejection gate: System rejected because no concrete requirement mentions real timeout classes/socket behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with two normal fake sources and one failing fake source; verify final report still renders, includes `data unavailable` only for that section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the real summary model writes (factual inclusion/accuracy), which a deterministic stub would hide. MOCK-HIDE: mocked generator would always pass representational-accuracy checks. MULTI-STEP: this is one model-output quality step, not a multi-agent sequence. Adversarial critique: if summary generation is purely template-based deterministic code, Agentic would be too high. TOP2-CONTRAST: runner-up Integration, but the behavior text uses 'accurately represents' narrative content, a model-output-quality criterion. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because no concrete text describes multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation with real model calls on fixed input fixtures containing salient numeric values; score output for factual coverage/correctness (e.g., required key-value mentions, no value inversion) across repeated runs with acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, which crosses network/email provider boundaries and cannot be proven by in-process fakes alone. MOCK-HIDE: mocked email sender could pass while SMTP/API auth, routing, or provider acceptance fails. LLM-DEP: behavior checks delivery correctness, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: although pipeline is end-to-end, it is not specified as multiple agentic decision stages. Adversarial critique: if a controlled local mail sink is considered sufficient, Integration might catch enough failures. TOP2-CONTRAST: runner-up Integration, but 'reaches ... inbox correctly' requires external delivery semantics beyond local module wiring. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because text does not require judging summary quality, only successful delivery outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script in an environment with real reachable source endpoints (or stable staging equivalents) and real email transport to a test mailbox; assert recipient inbox receives one correctly addressed report with expected structure/content sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: failure trigger is service unreachable (real network/service condition), plus persistence and retry behavior across runs. MOCK-HIDE: replacing email transport with a simple failing stub can miss real timeout/error-class behavior that gates fallback. Integration-BRAKE: (a) yes, behavior text includes real network unreachability, so Integration is insufficient without concrete disqualifier; (b) no AI-output dependency required; (c) no multi-agent chain. Adversarial critique: if the code treats all send failures uniformly, integration with injected exceptions could be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST: runner-up Integration, but the behavior explicitly anchors on unreachable delivery service and cross-run retry, making real infra the safer minimum to catch true outage handling defects. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because no multiple agentic steps are specified.
behaviors.test_description: In a system test, make email service genuinely unreachable (e.g., blocked endpoint or invalid route), run scheduled job once to verify report is persisted locally, then restore service and run next schedule cycle to verify queued report is retried and delivered.
